title: New Mexico Association of Disability Examiners (New MADE) — Constitution and By-Laws
description: Governing constitution and by-laws of the New Mexico Association of Disability Examiners (New MADE), based in Albuquerque and a chapter of the National Association of Disability Examiners (NADE) in the Southwest Region. Covers name, NADE affiliation, definitions, purpose, membership (revised 8/03), detailed finances and conference-funding distribution rules, the Executive Board, officers, extensive per-office 'charges' (duties) for President, President-Elect, Secretary, Treasurer, Past President and Board Members at Large, meetings, standing committees, and amendments.

source.publisher: National Association of Disability Examiners (NADE) — New Mexico Chapter (New MADE)
source.title: Constitution and By-Laws of the New Mexico Association of Disability Examiners (New MADE)

notes: Authoritative NADE chapter governing document. This is the markitdown-normalized markdown conversion of the sibling source file 'New-Mexico-Const-Bylaws.doc' (in the same directory); markdown formatting was machine-derived (the per-office 'Charges' sections render with restarting list numbering and stray heading markers as conversion artifacts). Article V (Membership) carries a '(Revised 8/03)' note, captured as temporal_coverage. NADE url is the organization site, not a deep link to this document.
